title: Patriots free agency live updates: Tracking New England’s top offseason pickups
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots have over $50 million in cap space for roster improvements after Super Bowl loss to Seattle.
New England released Stefon Diggs and traded center Garrett Bradbury to adjust their roster configuration.
NFL insider predicts Patriots to sign edge rusher Boye Mafe on a three-year, $48 million contract.

title: Boston Marathon runner aims to complete marathons in all 50 states
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Zara Harding, a 46-year-old from Henderson, Nevada, aims to complete marathons in all 50 states.
Since a 2021 cancer diagnosis, Harding has completed over a dozen marathons; after Boston, she'll have 10 states left.
Harding is running for the Dana-Farber Marathon Challenge Team, raising $12,000 for cancer research.

title: Judge dismisses lawsuit of Babson College student mistakenly deported to Honduras
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Judge Richard Stearns dismissed Any Lopez Belloza's habeas lawsuit after she declined a government flight to the U.S.
Lopez Belloza was mistakenly deported to Honduras, violating a court order, after detention at Logan Airport in November.
Her attorney claims ICE employs tactics hindering legal efforts, while a flight back is deemed a 'trap' by the legal team.

title: NBA coach dubs Derrick White as a ‘top-5 player’ in NBA
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Cleveland Cavaliers coach Kenny Atkinson calls Boston Celtics' Derrick White a 'top-five player' analytically in the NBA.
White is averaging 17.4 points, 4.4 rebounds, and 5.7 assists per game despite a shooting dip this season.
White's defensive versatility and playmaking remain pivotal for the Celtics as they integrate Jayson Tatum back from injury.

title: Jaylen Brown, Jayson Tatum dynamic shines in Celtics win over Cavaliers: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Jayson Tatum scored 20 points in Celtics' 109-98 victory against the Cavaliers, showing improvement in his recovery.
Jaylen Brown led the Celtics with 23 points, nine rebounds, and eight assists, highlighting his growth in Tatum's absence.
Celtics' defense contributed to Cavaliers' shooting struggles, as Cleveland shot poorly from beyond the arc in the second quarter.

title: Former Patriots WR cautions against signing Alec Pierce to a hefty contract
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Patriots are considering signing Colts wide receiver Alec Pierce, who led the NFL in yards per catch in 2024 and 2025.
Alec Pierce could command a salary of at least $27 million annually as a free agent this offseason.
Former Patriots WR Chris Hogan advised against spending $30 million annually for Pierce, highlighting the need for versatility.

title: North Kingstown, R.I. police officer dies by suicide, department says
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: North Kingstown Police Officer Brittany Dougal, age 29, died by suicide after battling mental illness, the department announced.
Dougal graduated from the Rhode Island Municipal Police Training Academy in December, starting her policing career in North Kingstown.
Family requests donations to the North Kingstown Animal Shelter or Tunnel to Towers Foundation in her memory.

title: 9 under-the-radar players for Patriots to consider in free agency
author: Conor Roche
bullets: The New England Patriots are considering under-the-radar free agents to enhance their roster this offseason.
Potential targets include Vikings receiver Jalen Nailor, Giants tight end Daniel Bellinger, and Bengals edge rusher Joseph Ossai.
Patriots have $60 million available, allowing flexibility to explore both big-name and lesser-known free agent additions.

title: Did Tom Brady prevent Patriots from acquiring Maxx Crosby? One report suggests possibility.
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Tom Brady, Raiders minority owner, reportedly influenced decision preventing Maxx Crosby's trade to the Patriots.
Raiders traded Maxx Crosby to Ravens for two first-round picks, including 14th overall in 2026 draft.
Patriots linked to edge rushers like Jaelan Phillips, Boye Mafe, and K’Lavon Chaisson in free agency moves.

title: Rumored Patriots target Alec Pierce reportedly expected to make at least $27M per year, might not hit free agency
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Alec Pierce, a top wide receiver, may demand a contract worth at least $27 million per year.
The Colts are attempting to re-sign Pierce, making him uncertain for free agency.
Pierce had 47 receptions, 1,003 yards, and six touchdowns in 2025 with the Colts.
